What about these damage suits
that are being filed by these men
because of alleged brutal treat
ment at the hands OF the police
and because of unsanitary condi.
lions in the city Jail? Do you
think that the taxpayers who must,
In fact, face these damage suits,
should not be considered little
more in this fight?" was the next
question.


"Of course these cases will have
to be tried out, l suppose, but
don't think the taxpayers need
worry over the payment of dam
ages. l don't believe the l. w. w.
will be able to win one of its
cases"


WIDELY SEPARATE OPINIONS


The ideas of the mayor and
those of the temporary leaders of
the |. w. w. are widely separated.
With the revolutionists. the fight
is still for free speech, and conf
dence is expressed of an ability to
collect damages from the city for
alleged misuse at the hands of the
police.


Revolutionary leaders declare
speech is not free so long as 1t is
confined to a certain place, in a
certain district of the city and at
certain time.
